cells.xml_text_content: Users with reasonable access1 to key services by mode of travel, England, 2007 to 2013
cells.pdf_text_content: Users with reasonable access1 to key services by mode of travel, England, 2007 to 2013

cells.xml_text_content: Number of people with reasonable access
cells.pdf_text_content: Number of people with reasonable access

cells.xml_text_content: Percentage of people with reasonable access
cells.pdf_text_content: Percentage of people with reasonable access

cells.xml_text_content: 1 The number/percentage of the population that can access a given services within a reasonable time. Please see the key definitions and terms note for further information on methods.
cells.pdf_text_content: 1 The number/percentage of the population that can access a given services within a reasonable time. Please see the key definitions and terms note for further information on methods.

cells.xml_text_content: 2 Users are the population of the local area for the relevant service, e.g. for primary schools, the users are children aged 5-10. Please see the guidance note for a full list of user groups.
cells.pdf_text_content: 2 Users are the population of the local area for the relevant service, e.g. for primary schools, the users are children aged 5-10. Please see the guidance note for a full list of user groups.

cells.xml_text_content: 3 The at risk users looks at particular social groups at risk of exclusion, e.g. for primary schools, the at risk users are children aged 5-10 known to be eligible for free school meals. Please see the guidance note for a full list of user groups.
cells.pdf_text_content: 3 The at risk users looks at particular social groups at risk of exclusion, e.g. for primary schools, the at risk users are children aged 5-10 known to be eligible for free school meals. Please see the guidance note for a full list of user groups.

cells.xml_text_content: 4 An at risk group is not measured for the Further Education service.
cells.pdf_text_content: 4 An at risk group is not measured for the Further Education service.

cells.xml_text_content: 5 A new method has been adopted in 2010 using Trafficmaster congestion data to calculate travel times for the car mode, as opposed to using default speeds. Please see the guidance note for further information.
cells.pdf_text_content: 5 A new method has been adopted in 2010 using Trafficmaster congestion data to calculate travel times for the car mode, as opposed to using default speeds. Please see the guidance note for further information.

cells.xml_text_content: 6 Public transport/walking data were not updated for 2012 due to a data quality issue. Please see 2013 statistical release or the guidance notes document for details.
cells.pdf_text_content: 6 Public transport/walking data were not updated for 2012 due to a data quality issue. Please see 2013 statistical release or the guidance notes document for details.

cells.xml_text_content: 7 A new method has been adopted in 2013 for the public transport/walking mode. Please see guidance notes document for details. The 2011 results have been recalculated on the new basis.
cells.pdf_text_content: 7 A new method has been adopted in 2013 for the public transport/walking mode. Please see guidance notes document for details. The 2011 results have been recalculated on the new basis.

cells.xml_text_content: The figures in this table are outside the scope of National Statistics
cells.pdf_text_content: The figures in this table are outside the scope of National Statistics
